page: Microsoft Azure E4ps_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Epsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores powered by a Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor operating at 3.4 GHz. The CPU architecture lacks simultaneous multithreading, providing exactly one thread per core. Memory configuration consists of 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Cache resources include 64 KB of L1 data cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and a large 128 MB L3 cache. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate a capable memory subsystem and solid single-threaded execution. The instance achieved a top-tier PassMark memory score of 2966.15, supported by a read-only small block memory bandwidth of 454.17 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression performance is strong at 33.04 GB/sec. However, cryptographic and extended instruction performance is weak to poor, with a PassMark encryption score of 750.01, primarily due to the absence of advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX, AMX) and SHA extensions, though AES hardware acceleration is present. General compute benchmarks, such as Geekbench (1681.0 single-core, 5515.0 multi-core) and stress-ng, place the server in the average tier.

Given its high memory density of 8.0 GB per vCPU and strong memory bandwidth, the E4ps_v6 offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require heavy vector mathematics or local disk storage. It is well-suited for relational databases, caching layers like Redis (achieving 8.3 million operations per second in SET tests), static web serving at 8.04 GB/sec, and software compilation. It is less suitable for machine learning inference or ray tracing due to weak vector instruction support and the lack of a GPU.
description: The Microsoft Azure E4ps_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Epsv6 family, configured with 4 dedicated physical cores on a 3.4 GHz Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a generous 8.0 GB per vCPU memory allocation, alongside a 128 MB L3 cache. While it lacks local storage and a GPU, it is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark results show a top-tier PassMark memory score and strong single-threaded gzip compression, though cryptographic and extended instruction performance is limited due to the absence of AVX and AMX extensions. This instance is highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and caching systems like Redis, offering a balanced, cost-efficient profile for non-vectorized computing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E4ps_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 32 GB of RAM, and a 128 MB L3 cache, optimized for memory-intensive workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E4ps_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine with 4 dedicated cores, 32 GB of RAM, and a 128 MB L3 cache, optimized for memory-intensive tasks.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine featuring high memory density and dedicated physical cores for memory-intensive workloads and database caching.
bullet_points: Powered by 4 dedicated Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 cores at 3.4 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB per vCPU
Features a large 128 MB L3 cache and top-tier memory bandwidth
Lacks local storage but supports Premium Storage integration
Best suited for database operations, Redis caching, and web serving
